destructiveStepGate: CEO-approved irreversible table-retirement decision after live tenant smoke and divergence proof

stages.action: Keep the bidirectional is_active mirror triggers in place so soft de-enrolls propagate between class_students and class_enrollments before any reader repoint.
Preserve the teacher SELECT policy on class_enrollments and the fail-closed historical reconcile that only removes stale class_students visibility.

Ran the read-only divergence report against the linked target DB before/with the boundary-reader repoint. The export found no direction-A leak rows, no direction-B authorization-widening rows, and no one-sided membership rows.
Repointed canAccessStudent teacher reachability away from class_students and onto the canonical class_enrollments policy boundary. The destructive legacy-table retirement remains CEO-gated.

Repointed teacher route-local roster checks that read class_students or named class_students as the teacher boundary, while preserving already-canonical class_enrollments routes. School-admin direct class_students writes remain tied to legacy sync/seat flows and are not teacher reachability boundaries.
evidence: class_enrollments is the Phase-2 canonical join table

stages.action: Closed the last residual RLS references to class_students: the public.students teacher backstop policy and the public.teacher_remediation_assignments teacher SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE policies now join through canonical class_enrollments instead of legacy class_students. class_students itself is untouched; only RLS join targets changed.
evidence: no-op on the effective visible row set

stages.action: Run the integration lane against staging after reader repoint so an assigned teacher sees canonical enrollments, a de-enrolled student stays hidden, and static tenant isolation is treated only as triage.
evidence: Use as a triage queue, not a verdict
